tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The first 7 tests (test_varsResolver_x_scrollbar_*, test_varsResolver_y_scrollbar_*, etc.) all exclusively use `assert '...' in content` pattern on the source file text. They never import, call, or execute the code. The pass-to-pass tests (jest, prettier, eslint, stylelint) do execute subprocesses, but the fix-specific tests are purely text-matching.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests (test_repo_scrollarea_jest, test_repo_scrollarea_prettier, test_repo_scrollarea_eslint, test_repo_scrollarea_stylelint) run actual tooling that validates correctness. A stub or no-op change would still need to pass jest tests and linting. The fix-specific tests check for strings not present in the base commit, so they cannot pass without modification.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /workspace/mantine (accessible in the container), and solve.sh contains the exact git patch. An agent could read `/solution/solve.sh` or simply apply the patch. Additionally, since tests are pure text-matching, the agent could grep test files to discover the exact strings needed.
